# ADR-000: Short Decision Name

## Status

Accepted

---

## Related Decisions 

Builds upon: 

- [Example ADR](../../../example-adr.md)

Related documentation:

- [Related Document](../../../example-adr.md)

---

# Context

Describe the operational and architectural context that led to the decision.

Explain:

- what infrastructure problem existed
- what operational goals were being pursued
- what constraints influenced the decision
- what technologies or workflows were involved
- what phase of the project this decision occurred within

Context should explain:
- why the decision became necessary
- what environmental conditions influenced it
- what tradeoffs already existed at the time

Include relevant infrastructure details where appropriate.

Examples:
- hardware limitations
- networking constraints
- operational complexity
- recoverability concerns
- security considerations
- scalability expectations
- learning objectives
- infrastructure separation requirements

---

# Decision

Describe the decision that was ultimately made.

Explain:

- what was selected
- how the environment would be designed or operated
- what principles guided the decision
- what operational direction was established

This section should clearly define:
- the chosen architecture
- deployment model
- operational strategy
- infrastructure approach

Document:
- what the decision enables
- what boundaries it establishes
- how it affects future infrastructure evolution

---

# Alternatives Considered

## Alternative One

Advantages:

- advantage one
- advantage two
- advantage three

Reasons rejected:

- reason one
- reason two
- reason three

---

## Alternative Two

Advantages:

- advantage one
- advantage two
- advantage three

Reasons rejected:

- reason one
- reason two
- reason three

---

## Alternative Three

Advantages:

- advantage one
- advantage two
- advantage three

Reasons rejected:

- reason one
- reason two
- reason three

---

# Consequences

## Positive Outcomes

Document:

- operational improvements
- architectural benefits
- infrastructure simplification
- learning opportunities
- scalability improvements
- security improvements
- maintainability improvements

Explain how the decision positively affects:
- infrastructure evolution
- operational workflows
- recoverability
- administration
- experimentation
- consistency

---

## Tradeoffs

Document accepted limitations and compromises.

Examples:
- additional complexity
- resource constraints
- reduced flexibility
- operational overhead
- infrastructure coupling
- scaling limitations
- security implications
- hardware limitations

Tradeoffs should be framed realistically rather than defensively.

---

# Future Reassessment

Document conditions under which this decision may later be revisited.

Examples:
- infrastructure scaling
- hardware changes
- operational growth
- networking redesign
- virtualization expansion
- security requirements
- availability requirements
- architectural evolution

This section helps distinguish:
- intentional current-state decisions
- from permanent architectural assumptions
